critic_raw: The missingness indicators concern me primarily because income documentation absence could reflect a manual review request that comes during the application process rather than applicant-initiated non-submission. If the documentation request is triggered by an underwriter flag during the same application session, the absence would be recorded at a time that depends on the underwriting decision — making it post-decision.
defender_raw: The missingness indicator captures whether documentation was present in the initial application submission packet, not whether documentation was subsequently requested and not provided. An applicant who submits an application without income documentation is recorded as missing-income at submission time, before any underwriter sees the file. If an underwriter later requests additional documentation, that is a separate event that is not captured by the submission-time indicator. The timestamp confirms the indicator is set at submission.

debate_rounds.critic: The distinction between submission-time missingness and request-triggered missingness is the correct analysis. If the indicator is set at initial submission before any underwriter interaction, it cannot be contaminated by the underwriting decision. I accept this. The timestamp audit confirming submission-time derivation is the correct verification mechanism. I concede the leakage concern.
debate_rounds.defender: Agreed. Submission-time missingness is a genuine applicant behavior signal — applicants who do not provide documentation at submission differ in credit risk profile from those who do, regardless of what happens during underwriting. The indicator is both available at decision time and genuinely predictive.

adjudication_raw: The critic correctly concedes after one round when the submission-time vs. request-triggered distinction is clarified. Defense wins.
all_issues_raised: submission-time vs request-triggered missingness concern — conceded when distinction clarified

empirical_test.condition: Audit timestamp for each missingness indicator to confirm set at submission; verify no request-triggered or post-decision missingness is encoded; confirm future-quarter frozen-model evaluation
empirical_test.supports_critique_if: Any missingness indicator timestamped after initial submission or dependent on underwriter action
empirical_test.supports_defense_if: All indicators timestamped at submission; no post-decision information; future-quarter holdout confirms performance
empirical_test.ambiguous_if: Most indicators are clearly submission-time but edge cases in documentation workflow create uncertainty for some fields
